title: Expressing support for the designation of the month of January, as "Tamil Language and Heritage Month".
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
plain_summary: This bill asks Congress to make January "Tamil Language and Heritage Month" every year. Tamil is one of the world's oldest languages spoken by people from places like India and Sri Lanka. Many Tamil Americans live in the United States and help make our country more diverse with their culture and traditions. The bill wants to celebrate their language and the good things they bring to America, especially during January when they celebrate their harvest festival called Pongal.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.

sections.title: Resolution Expressing Support for Tamil Language and Heritage Month
sections.text: This resolution shows support for making January the month to celebrate Tamil language and culture. Tamil is one of the oldest languages still used today, with records going back over two thousand six hundred years. Recent digging in Tamil Nadu, India has found proof of an advanced city from the third century before the common era. Tamil is an official language in three countries and over eighty million people worldwide speak it. About three hundred sixty thousand Tamil speakers live in the United States as of two thousand twenty three. Tamil communities celebrate the Pongal Festival each year in mid January. This festival marks the Tamil month called Thai and celebrates the harvest, cattle, and Tamil arts. This adds to the rich mix of cultures in the United States. Tamil people, their culture, traditions, and language have affected many parts of American economic, social, and cultural history. Famous Tamil books include the Thirukkural, Tholkappiyam, Aganaanuru, Puranaanuru, Silappatikaram, and Manimegalai. These books contain lasting values that inspire Tamil culture and make global heritage richer. Modern Tamil people can still read and understand these old works. They have been translated into more than one hundred languages and their values still matter today. Making January Tamil Language and Heritage Month lets Americans celebrate the rich history, language, and culture of Tamil people. It also encourages all Americans to learn about how Tamil Americans have contributed to our nation's cultural fabric. States like Michigan, Georgia, and Massachusetts have made official statements to celebrate Tamil people's cultural contributions. States like North Carolina, Wisconsin, Virginia, and Minnesota have recognized January as Tamil Heritage Month. This shows that appreciation for Tamil culture is growing across the United States. The House of Representatives supports making January Tamil Language and Heritage Month. It recognizes that Tamil Americans make the United States more diverse and stronger. It also acknowledges that Pongal is an important harvest celebration for Tamil people.
